» I.R.I.S. «
[[File:I.R.I.S. Logo.png|thumb|center|500px]]
{{Quote|Infiltrate. Manipulate. Sabotage.|I.R.I.S. Motto}}
»I-INTRO«
The '''Insurgent Rogueware and Infiltration Syndicate''', or more simply referred to as '''I.R.I.S''', is a group within '''The Backrooms''' founded in late-2017. '''I.R.I.S''' presents itself as a highly secretive hacking unit, dedicated to deploying specialized malware in order to obtain, manipulate, and extract valuable information and data from existing systems in '''The Backrooms'''. '''I.R.I.S''' currently has a population of approximately 350 members, a number that continues to grow over time. Its personnel reside within '''Base Pupil''', the group’s official headquarters. Initiate members must undergo a rigorous evaluation process, assessing their technical proficiency and aptitude in cyberspace. Despite their seemingly malicious exterior, members of the group remain neutral toward outside wanderers. Hostility is only shown toward individuals who attempt to access restricted data without authorization or pose a direct threat to '''Base Pupil'''. Members of '''I.R.I.S''' carry encrypted identification tokens embedded into their clothing, which serve as the primary method of verification. All '''Initiates''' who successfully join the group undergo this process, and are registered within a secured internal database of authorized personnel. Following an attempted breach in 2019, '''I.R.I.S's''' bases became heavily fortified with advanced defense systems, including automated security measures, surveillance drones, and biometric scanners. In the event of a confirmed breach, a base-wide lockdown is initiated, activating electrified flooring in restricted zones and automated turrets equipped with thermal imaging to neutralize intruders. While '''I.R.I.S.''' is primarily a hacking organization, its operations are not conducted for activist or overtly malicious purposes. Small-scale operations, typically limited to information retrieval, are carried out with caution; accidental infections of civilian terminals are remotely mitigated as soon as possible. Though their methods may appear sinister, '''I.R.I.S.''' does not intentionally cause widespread collateral damage to uninvolved wanderers or their equipment.
»II-TECH«
Alongside being a hacking group, '''I.R.I.S''' is also known for developing multiple advanced and remarkable technologies over the years. A few of these advancements are described below.
Thermal-Imaging Surveillance Drones
{{Underline|FF0000}} '''Thermal-Imaging Surveillance Drones''' were developed by '''I.R.I.S.''' to enhance base security. These drones come in the shape of a disk with a sphere in the middle, similar to a flying saucer. Their motors allow for quiet surveillance in their base. They patrol designated areas, using thermal imaging to scan humans passing under its range.
»III-HIERARCHY«
At the top of the Hierarchy lies The Eye, the name given to the founder of I.R.I.S. They are in charge of overseeing everything that goes on in the group, and setting the foundation of the group's ideals and vision. In the event that The Eye either resigns or dies through whatever means, another person is carefully selected as the heir to their position. Though, thankfully, such an event has not happened, as The Eye is almost never seen outside Base Pupil. The rest of the hierarchy is as followed: ◇ Lead Strategists: Considered the right hand assistants of The Eye, they are responsible for planning large scale operations and overseeing all squadrons within the group. ◇ Cell Coordinators: Leaders of each individual squadron, they are responsible for directing their respective team in times of operation or otherwise daily tasks. In more routine and smaller operations, they are mainly in charge of planning them instead. ◇ Data Manipulators: These individuals are in charge of extracting and organizing the data gathered from operations. ◇ Technical Programmers-Handles a majority of tasks involving developing new malware or other technologies. ◇ Mechanical Engineer-Provide crucial support in fixing and maintaining technological systems within the base. ◇ Supplier: Often at the same ranking level as Recruits, they are responsible for scavenging available supplies within Level 4.3, concentrating them into the base, and keeping track. ◇ Recruits: Being basic members of I.R.I.S, these individuals ◇ Initiates: Potential candidates undergoing evaluation. It is possible that, during an individual's time in I.R.I.S, that they may have the ability to rank up in the hierarchy. However, such a process is not easy, and one must demonstrate that they have the sufficient capabilities of the rank to which they are trying to achieve.
»IV-OUTPOSTS«
»V-SUPPLYING«
With '''Base Pupil''' located in '''[[Level 4.3]]''', '''I.R.I.S.''' has access to abundant naturally regenerating resources such as food and water. Small scout teams are sent from the base to gather these supplies and transport them to designated storage rooms. However, while this method works, relying solely on it presents challenges, as the group must not only concentrate these resources into their base, but also scavenge enough to sustain all 350 members. It is also believed that '''I.R.I.S.''' occasionally works with third-party groups, either during conflicts or times of general need. These partnerships are typically symbiotic, with '''I.R.I.S.''' completing missions in exchange for a substantial supply of resources.
»VI-HISTORY«
I.R.I.S began in late 2017, where an anonymous individual had founded the group with the vision of establishing a dominant technical and cyberspatial presence in The Backrooms. This small group had decided to establish their base of operations in Level 4.3, for reasons such as easy access to several computer parts, safety from hostile entities, and proximity to various resources such as food. After deciding the location, the base slowly began construction, and was finished in mere weeks after the group's founding. It would eventually become known as Base Pupil. In the early months after Base Pupil was established, the group preferred to keep themselves relatively hidden, not drawing attention from larger groups. '''I.R.I.S''' originally aimed at creating intricate computer networks and technological systems. Their goals were unclear at the time, but their motives were far different from now. At the time, the group was still small enough to where they could sustain themselves via the food and supplies found within '''Level 4.3'''. At the time, Base Pupil had fairly lower level security measures, and protocols were less strict, possibly from the fact that they had managed to keep themselves relatively hidden. This would eventually backfire on them. In March 2020, '''Base Pupil''' had experienced a breach by a small group of unknown wanderers. They were able to sneak through the lower level security measures and even retrieve a protected document. However, upon being notified, the group of intruders was quickly apprehended before they could reach far into the base. They were identified to be spies from an unknown group, likely aiming to capture confidential information about '''I.R.I.S''' and sell it to others. They were quickly disposed of, and the stolen document was retrieved. This single event has fueled '''Base Pupil''' to drastically increase their security measures, as well as add stricter protocols and procedures; they wanted to make sure such an incident like this would never happen again. This event also marked the turning point for '''I.R.I.S's''' ideals. As aforementioned, their original focus was simply creating advanced computer networks and systems. However, they started to shift their focus toward intelligence operations, prioritizing the analysis and manipulation of information. '''I.R.I.S''' came to believe that information itself was the most valuable aspect of surviving in The Backrooms, and controlling its flow could grant it a significant advantage over other groups. In the years following this shift, '''I.R.I.S.''' began developing increasingly complex malware to autonomously gather and process large quantities of information. While many have proven effective, certain experimental programs produced unintended results that were poorly understood. Among these, one particular project eventually went rogue, creating an anomalous software entity that now roams The Backrooms.

'''> Close Log <'''
|body=
My name is [CLASSIFIED], one of the '''Lead Strategists''' at '''I.R.I.S.''' I often see people think of us as a group of soulless, evil villanous individuals that enjoy deploying malware for the sake of chaos and to cause suffering. The simple truth is, that's not the case. We are not like [[Recovered_Data_File|those unhuman fools]]. While our methods might seem ruthless and sinister, we operate them with a reason. Our actions at '''I.R.I.S''' are very... ''complicated'' to say the very least. '''The Backrooms''' is a harsh, hellish place. It's full of [[Entity List|danger]] and [[Phenomenon 12|unpredictability]], and you never know what comes next. The environment around us is changing, and we have to adapt. We are not soulless individuals with a complete lack of morality. While morale at '''I.R.I.S''' is grey, we have a boundary between offense for the sake of survival, and causing outright suffering to innocent wanderers. We aren't evil villains, but a community of people that are trapped in an endless realm that leave us no choice but to do the actions that we do to sustain ourselves. After all, You have to do what you can to survive...

WRITING GUIDE FOR PEOPLE WHO WANT TO MAKE PAGES ABOUT IRIS. -IRIS isn't an outwardly evil group. They don't intend to cause widespread harm and chaos to innocent wanderers terminals, and if they do, they attempt to mitigate it. There is a line between doing an offense for survival and causing outright chaos and suffering. -IRIS doesn't have extremely complex malware and futuristic technology. Though they are a more advanced group, it still has to be in the general realm of realism. -Pages portraying IRIS should align with its established lore. Avoid straying too far away from it, as it could create confusion. -Not every member at IRIS is a genius hacker. While that is a general majority, some members are more skilled in other backgrounds, like strategy and engineering. }}
